primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of foreign exchange variation on intragroup dividend receivables for designation as hedged item in consolidated financial statements under hedge accounting rules

primary_authority.reason: Paragraph 6.3.5 establishes the general rule that hedge accounting applies only to external party transactions in consolidated financial statements, while paragraph 6.3.6 provides the exception for intragroup monetary items with foreign currency risk affecting consolidated profit or loss

supporting_authority.reason: Paragraph 32 addresses exchange differences on monetary items forming part of net investment in foreign operations, potentially providing an alternative treatment pathway for certain intragroup dividend receivables
Clarifies requirements for net investment hedges including that hedging instruments may be held by any entity within the group and effectiveness assessment methodology

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses non-cash asset distributions but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility for foreign exchange variation
Addresses cash flow classification of dividends paid/received, not hedge accounting designation eligibility
Addresses recognition and presentation of gains/losses on financial instruments, not hedge accounting eligibility criteria

treatment_families.family: General intragroup transaction restriction: intragroup monetary items ineligible for hedge designation in consolidated financial statements
Foreign currency risk exception: intragroup monetary items may qualify when foreign exchange exposure affects consolidated profit or loss
Net investment hedge pathway: monetary items forming part of net investment in foreign operation with alternative OCI treatment

approaches.label: Intragroup transaction hedge prohibition
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9.6.3.5, hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements applies only to external party transactions, making intragroup dividend receivables generally ineligible for hedge designation

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item hedge exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9.6.3.6, foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items may qualify as a hedged item when exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation and will affect consolidated profit or loss

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IAS 21.32, exchange differences on monetary items forming part of net investment in foreign operations are recognised initially in OCI rather than profit or loss, potentially qualifying for hedge designation under IFRIC 16
